name: Object Detection Model Performance Trade-offs
description:
This visualization compares state-of-the-art object detection models across three critical dimensions: Accuracy (mAP@0.5:0.95), Inference Latency (ms), and Model Size (MB).

The chart highlights the Pareto Frontier of computer vision—the boundary where you cannot improve one metric without sacrificing another. High-performance models like RT-DETR and YOLOv8 aim for the bottom-right corner (High Accuracy, Low Latency), while older architectures like Faster R-CNN show higher latency for similar accuracy levels. The marker size represents the computational complexity (FLOPs), illustrating the resource intensity required for specific architectures.

plotLayout.title: mAP vs. Latency vs. Model Size Benchmarking
